# pangolin_resource_header_auth (Resource)

Sets header-based authentication for a Pangolin HTTP resource. Pangolin injects an `Authorization` header into proxied requests. Destroying this resource removes the header authentication.

> **Note:** Credentials cannot be read back from the Pangolin API. After import, you must set `password` and `user` manually to avoid drift detection issues.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "pangolin_resource_header_auth" "example" {
  resource_id            = pangolin_resource.example.id
  user                   = "admin"
  password               = var.header_auth_password
  extended_compatibility = false
}
```

## Import

```shell
terraform import pangolin_resource_header_auth.example <resource_id>
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `password` (String, Sensitive) The password for header authentication.
- `resource_id` (Number, Forces Replace) The ID of the resource to protect with header authentication.
- `user` (String) The username for header authentication.

### Optional

- `extended_compatibility` (Boolean) Whether to enable extended compatibility mode. Defaults to `false`.
